questions.q: By what Hindi name is the Indian National Army also widely known?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Army was popularly called the Azad Hind Fauj, meaning the Free India Army.

questions.q: Which leader revitalized and led the Indian National Army in 1943?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose took over and reorganized the Indian National Army in 1943.

questions.q: In which broad region was the Indian National Army organized?
questions.explanation: The INA was organized among Indians in Southeast Asia to fight for India's independence.

questions.q: What was the primary aim of the Indian National Army?
options: To defend British India from invasion
To establish a monarchy in India
To fight for India's independence from British rule
questions.explanation: The INA was formed to fight for the liberation of India from British colonial rule.

questions.q: The title 'Netaji' is most closely associated with which INA leader?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose, who led the INA, was popularly addressed as 'Netaji,' meaning respected leader.

questions.q: Which famous slogan is attributed to Subhas Chandra Bose and the INA?
options: Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom
questions.explanation: Bose gave the call 'Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom' to inspire INA soldiers.

questions.q: The soldiers who first formed the INA were largely drawn from which group?
options: Indian prisoners of war captured from the British army
questions.explanation: The INA was largely formed from Indian soldiers of the British Indian Army taken prisoner by Japan.

questions.q: During which global conflict did the Indian National Army carry out its main campaigns?
questions.explanation: The INA fought during the Second World War, alongside Japan against the British in the 1940s.

questions.q: The struggle of the Indian National Army had what notable effect within India?
options: It inspired strong nationalist sentiment at home
It had no impact in India
questions.explanation: News of the INA's struggle stirred powerful nationalist feeling across India.

questions.q: The greeting 'Jai Hind' is popularly linked with which movement or force?
questions.explanation: 'Jai Hind' was popularized as a salutation by the Indian National Army under Bose.

questions.q: Who is credited with first organizing the Indian National Army in 1942, before Bose took charge?
questions.explanation: Captain Mohan Singh formed the first INA in 1942 from Indian POWs, before it was reorganized under Bose.

questions.q: Which veteran revolutionary, long resident in Japan, helped lay the groundwork for the INA before handing leadership to Bose?
questions.explanation: Rash Behari Bose helped establish the Indian Independence League and INA, later passing leadership to Subhas Chandra Bose.

questions.q: The provisional government associated with the INA, proclaimed in 1943, was called what?
options: Azad Hind (Provisional Government of Free India)
questions.explanation: Bose proclaimed the Arzi Hukumat-e-Azad Hind, the Provisional Government of Free India, in October 1943.

questions.q: In which city did Subhas Chandra Bose proclaim the Provisional Government of Free India in October 1943?
questions.explanation: Bose announced the Provisional Government of Free India in Singapore on 21 October 1943.

questions.q: Which foreign power allied with the INA during its campaigns in Southeast Asia?
questions.explanation: The INA fought alongside Imperial Japan against the British during the Second World War.

questions.q: The INA, alongside Japanese forces, took part in a major 1944 campaign aimed at entering India through which region?
options: Imphal and Kohima in the northeast
questions.explanation: In 1944 the INA joined the Japanese offensive toward Imphal and Kohima on India's northeastern frontier.

questions.q: Which island territory did the Provisional Government of Free India claim authority over after Japan transferred it in 1943?
questions.explanation: Japan handed nominal control of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands to the Azad Hind government in 1943.

questions.q: The INA's regiments were named after major national figures. Which of the following was an INA brigade or regiment name?
questions.explanation: INA units bore nationalist names such as the Gandhi Brigade, Azad Brigade and Nehru Brigade.

questions.q: What was the salutation popularized by the INA that later became a common patriotic greeting in India?
questions.explanation: The INA popularized 'Jai Hind' (Victory to India), which endured as a patriotic greeting after independence.

questions.q: After Japan's surrender in 1945, what happened to the Indian National Army as a fighting force?
options: It collapsed and many soldiers were taken prisoner
It merged with the British Indian Army
questions.explanation: With Japan's defeat in 1945 the INA disintegrated, and many of its soldiers were captured by the British.

questions.q: Before leading the INA in Asia, Subhas Chandra Bose had organized an Indian unit in Europe alongside which power?
questions.explanation: Bose first raised the Indian Legion in Germany before travelling to Southeast Asia to lead the INA.

questions.q: By what dramatic means did Subhas Chandra Bose travel from Germany to Southeast Asia in 1943?
questions.explanation: Bose made the journey from Germany to Asia in 1943 by submarine, transferring between German and Japanese vessels.

questions.q: What was the name of the broader civilian organization in Southeast Asia from which the INA drew much of its support?
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence League mobilized the Indian diaspora in Southeast Asia in support of the INA.

questions.q: The first INA under Mohan Singh was dissolved in late 1942 chiefly because of what?
options: Disputes over Japanese control and treatment of the army
questions.explanation: The first INA was disbanded in December 1942 amid friction with the Japanese over the army's autonomy and role.

questions.q: On Indian soil during the 1944 advance, the INA is recorded as raising the national flag at which border location?
questions.explanation: The INA hoisted the tricolour at Moirang in Manipur in April 1944, one of the few spots of Indian soil it reached.

questions.q: The INA's offensive toward Imphal in 1944 ultimately failed largely due to which factors?
options: A mutiny by all its officers
Monsoon conditions, supply shortages and a British counteroffensive
An outbreak of plague in Singapore
questions.explanation: The Imphal-Kohima campaign collapsed amid the monsoon, severe supply problems, and a successful British counteroffensive.

questions.q: What anthem did the Provisional Government of Free India and the INA adopt?
options: A version of 'Subh Sukh Chain' based on Tagore's song
questions.explanation: The INA adopted 'Subh Sukh Chain,' an adaptation of Rabindranath Tagore's song, as its anthem.

questions.q: The widely reported death of Subhas Chandra Bose in 1945 is associated with what event?
options: A plane crash in Taiwan (then Formosa)
questions.explanation: Bose is widely reported to have died in a plane crash at Taihoku (Taipei) in August 1945, though some dispute the account.

questions.q: Several countries' Indian communities funded the INA. Bose's call for funds and recruits was especially strong among Indians in which countries?
questions.explanation: The Indian communities of Malaya and Burma provided crucial money and recruits for Bose's INA.

questions.q: The captured INA officers were later put on trial by the British at which historic location in 1945-46?
options: The Cellular Jail in the Andamans
questions.explanation: Captured INA officers were court-martialled at the Red Fort in Delhi in the celebrated INA trials of 1945-46.
